fr:frontmatter.fr:title: A Cartesian closed category of event structures with quotients

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We introduce a new class of morphisms for event structures. The category obtained is cartesian closed, and a natural notion of quotient event structure is defined within it. We study in particular the topological space of maximal configurations of quotient event structures. We introduce the compression of event structures as an example of quotient: the compression of an event structure E is a minimal event structure with the same space of maximal configurations as E.

An event structure is defined to be triple { \mathopen {} \left ( E, \mathsf { Con } _{ E } , \leq _E \right ) \mathclose {}} where E is a partial order of events partially ordered by the causal dependency relation \leq _E, and \mathsf { Con } _{ E } \subseteq \mathcal {P} _{ \mathit {fin} } { \mathopen {} \left ( E \right ) \mathclose {}} specifies when a finite subset of E is consistent, satisfying the following laws:

fr:ol.fr:li: every downset of E is finite;
any subset of a consistent subset is consistent;
for any consistent subset x \in \mathsf { Con } _{ E } and e \leq e' with e' \in x, we have x \cup { \mathopen {} \left \{ e \right \} \mathclose {}} \in \mathsf { Con } _{ E }.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Event structures and prime algebraic domains

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Event structures are the same thing as prime algebraic domains; on objects, this has been proved many times, but I could only find the correct notion of morphism in a paper of Abbes.
